title: Harden exact live-preflight preparation contracts
goal: Eliminate the argument-adapter collision and align the Lenskit preparation outputs with the exact Grabowski live-runner contract without starting any provider process.

approval.note: Provider use is forbidden. Fix and verify contracts synthetically before any new live authorization is considered.

acceptance.assertion: The outer live-preflight argument parser disables option abbreviation, forwards an explicit absolute --claude-command unchanged and rejects ambiguous or prefix-only spellings.

A provider-free contract test exercises the exact production live argv through the outer adapter and proves the absolute executable, SHA binding, credential boundary, budgets and output paths reach the core without a provider intent.
Lenskit planning emits or rejects provider and sampling values according to the exact Claude-Code live-runner contract, including provider anthropic-claude-code and an explicit empty sampling object.
The agent-portable export path makes secret redaction an explicit machine-verifiable requirement or safe default before a benchmark bundle can be accepted.
Focused and full tests, Python 3.10 and 3.12 CI, diff-bound self-review and post-merge readback pass with zero provider processes and zero provider cost.

known_findings: Lenskit planner initially accepted generic anthropic provider and non-empty sampling rejected by the live runner
agent-portable snapshot creation required explicit --redact-secrets to pass the export gate
does_not_establish: permission for a new live pair
metadata.outcome: The exact live-preflight preparation chain is hardened and verified without provider use. Lenskit now rejects non-executable Claude-Code runner plans and applies required secret redaction safely before export; Grabowski preserves the exact Claude executable argument, rejects argparse abbreviations at both parser layers and accepts only the known optional execution contract. Both repositories were merged, post-merge checks passed, and the healthy live Grabowski runtime contains the merged hardening. No provider process was started and no RepoBrief usefulness is claimed.

runtime_readback.ancestry_evidence: GitHub compare reports live head one commit ahead of the T002E Grabowski merge.

verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t002e-no-prefix-abbrev: Both the outer adapter and inner preflight argparse parsers set allow_abbrev=false; focused tests prove exact --claude-command forwarding and rejection of prefix spellings.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t002e-exact-live-argv-test: The final provider-free cross-repository simulation passed the exact production argument path with one synthetic baseline and treatment intent, zero provider intents and unchanged sources.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t002e-plan-contract: Lenskit binds grabowski-claude-code-live-v1, requires anthropic-claude-code and an explicitly present empty sampling object, and rejects ambiguous or unsupported configurations before planning.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t002e-export-contract: Profiles whose canonical policy requires redaction enable it by default; an explicit disable is rejected before output creation and the result reports the redaction decision.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t002e-regression: Full local suites, repository gates, exact GitHub diff reviews, PR CI, post-merge tree readback, post-merge CI and live runtime ancestry all passed with zero provider processes and zero provider cost.

control_plane_followup.finding: A foreign repository lease was acquired after the initial live-state check but before PR #195 merge dispatch. The merged code and evidence remained valid, but the lease snapshot was not atomically refreshed immediately before the merge effect.
control_plane_followup.impact_on_t002e_result: none on code correctness, provider safety or runtime validity; process-ordering control requires separate hardening

next_task.blocker: A fresh live pair still requires a new separate reviewed authorization. No T002D path, authorization or evidence may be reused.

live_provider_evidence.reason: T002E is provider-free contract hardening and verification only.
